errors: The "ProcedureLookBack" function uses a 1-month lookback period for colonoscopy procedures, which is clinically unreasonable. Colon cancer screening guidelines (e.g., USPSTF) typically recommend colonoscopy every 10 years for average-risk adults. The lookback period should align with this interval (e.g., 10 years) to avoid recommending unnecessary procedures or missing due screenings.
["HbA1c threshold of 9% for diabetes diagnosis exceeds standard clinical guidelines (ADA recommends ≥6.5%)"]

["The ELM does not include a check for 20 pack-year smoking history, which is a key criterion in USPSTF lung cancer screening guidelines. The 'Has Smoking History' condition only checks for active tobacco use status, not duration or pack-year calculation."]

[Minimum Age is set to 60-75 years, but clinical guidelines (e.g., USPSTF) recommend colorectal cancer screening starting at age 50. Recent screening interval is defined as 2 years, but standard intervals vary by modality (e.g., colonoscopy every 10 years). These parameters may lead to incorrect inclusion/exclusion of patients.]

["In Demographic" expression uses an age threshold of 30 years, which is inconsistent with clinical guidelines (e.g., CDC recommends HIV screening for all individuals aged 13-64, with specific high-risk populations screened more frequently). This restrictive age cutoff may exclude younger patients who should be screened according to standard recommendations.]
["In Demographic" expression incorrectly checks age >=30 instead of age >=21 per USPSTF guidelines. Cervical cancer screening recommendations start at age 21, not 30, for women aged 21-65 years.]

["In Demographic" expression uses an age threshold of 75 years, but USPSTF guidelines recommend falls prevention screening for community-dwelling adults aged 65 years or older at increased risk. The age cutoff in the ELM is more restrictive than the clinical guideline.]

["In Demographic" expression uses an age threshold of 25 years, but USPSTF guidelines recommend screening for unhealthy alcohol use in adults aged 18 years or older. This discrepancy creates a clinically unreasonable restriction that excludes patients aged 18-24 from the target population.]
["In Demographic age range is 55-75 years, but USPSTF recommends 65-75 years for AAA screening. The lower age threshold of 55 is clinically unreasonable."]

["Needs Tobacco Use Screening" expression only checks demographic (age ≥18) but does not exclude patients with recent tobacco screening (within past year). The clinical logic should require both age ≥18 AND no recent screening to trigger the recommendation, but the current implementation incorrectly treats "Needs Screening" as equivalent to "In Demographic" alone. This would result in recommending annual screening regardless of prior screenings, violating the intended annual screening guideline."]
["In Demographic" expression only checks age >=50 but USPSTF guidelines specify 50-80 years. Missing upper age limit of 80 in demographic criteria.]

["Needs Falls Prevention Screening" logic incorrectly requires both demographic eligibility and a recent falls assessment. The clinical guideline intends to recommend screening for patients in the demographic who do NOT have a recent assessment. Additionally, the ELM does not check the result of the falls assessment (e.g., increased risk) before recommending intervention, which is required per USPSTF guidelines.]
